title: cerastium alpinum var. latifolium
intro: Cerastium alpinum var. latifolium, also known as the alpine whitlowgrass, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum var. latifolium include its ability to accelerate wound healing, reduce inflammation, and prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
